entries.title: SkillForge: agents that auto-detect their own skills
entries.bodyMd: AST-based retrieval over a Claude Skill library, human-gated approval loop. Cuts repeat-class error context from **~3000 → ~80 tokens (~50×)**. Repo: [github.com/Allmight-456/SkillForge](https://github.com/Allmight-456).

entries.title: Hermes & OpenClaw on Hostinger cloud
entries.bodyMd: Self-hosted long-running agent boxes, kept alive specifically to watch where production-grade agent loops degrade. Tool-loop convergence, memory bleed across runs, where they crack under sustained load. Operational notes feed back into SkillForge.

entries.title: guardrails, evals, agent loops
entries.bodyMd: Wiring them *into* the LLM workflow itself, not bolted on after. The open question: where do evals belong — pre-tool, post-tool, or as a separate critic agent?

entries.title: mem0 vs SkillForge
entries.bodyMd: [mem0](https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0) for long-horizon memory. Mapping the gap between mem0 (episodic, vector-backed) and SkillForge (procedural, AST-indexed) — different halves of the same problem.

entries.title: PageIndex & Agentic RAG
entries.bodyMd: [PageIndex](https://github.com/VectifyAI/PageIndex) and tree-structured retrieval — moving past vector-only. Reads more like how you'd actually search a codebase or a textbook.

entries.title: Conductor.build & Superset side-by-side
entries.bodyMd: Running them against real PRs to see which agentic loop converges. Cursor stays the daily driver; these are the challengers.

entries.title: Karpathy's nanochat & Autoresearch
entries.bodyMd: Single-GPU nanochat lineage. Reference for what "tiny but real" looks like at the model layer.
